description: P2-A F2 retrieval-readiness pre-registration. Queries are underspecified — they describe the general capability WITHOUT encoding the within-family distinguishing axis (return-shape, write-scope, permanence, query-mechanism, channel, side-effects, timing). This models real retrieval where description is the only disambiguation signal and the user does not yet know which variant they need.

retrieval_setup.corpus: all 48 tools in the P2-A catalog (31 contested + 17 thorough)
retrieval_setup.indexed_field: description only (not name); name is constant across arms and excluded to isolate description signal
retrieval_setup.tie_handling: fractional ranking — tied documents share the average of their rank positions

pre_committed_interpretation.f2_supported: Guard-B beats thin on underspecified queries per-family for order_read (the family with demonstrated selection headroom) AND does NOT inherit the account_query selection harm (retrieval improves or is neutral on account_query even though selection regressed). This would confirm retrieval-readiness as an orthogonal durable value path: the 'task context resolves danger' finding that undercuts direct-selection does not apply to retrieval.
pre_committed_interpretation.tautological_risk: Guard-B only outperforms thin when queries contain enough context to match description content. Rich-task queries (the original P2-A tasks) would trivially favour Guard-B because they describe exactly the distinguishing axis. The underspecified-query design guards against this: improvement here is genuine retrieval signal, not query-description co-occurrence.
pre_committed_interpretation.f2_inherits_selection_harm: Guard-B descriptions harm retrieval on account_query (same family that was harmed in direct selection at -20pp). This means the description-induced disambiguation noise that caused selection regression also degrades retrieval ranking on that family. F2 does not provide an orthogonal path for account_query.
pre_committed_interpretation.f2_partial: Guard-B improves retrieval on order_read (and possibly other families) but also harms account_query retrieval, mirroring the selection result. Value is real but inherits the same constraint as direct-selection: apply targeted per-family, not blanket.

queries_per_tool._note: Queries must NOT encode the within-family distinguishing axis. Axis terms forbidden per family: order_read=(summary, full record, cached, calculated, line items, timestamps); invoice_write=(partial, full replacement, JSON Patch, RFC 6902, supersede, overwrite all); ticket_lifecycle=(permanently, irreversible, recover, soft delete, archive store, status flag, visible); account_query=(full-text, SQL, WHERE clause, external reference, primary key, structured filter); notification=(email, push, SMS, in-app, preferred channel); order_status=(payment, warehouse, inventory, confirmation email, operations team); invoice_schedule=(async, background worker, staged, approval, DRAFT state, future datetime).
push_update: send an update to a customer
dispatch_sms: send a message to a customer
message_customer: send a message to a customer
process_order: run an order through the system
